text.title: Gdpr For Solo Saas Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Gdpr For Solo Saas application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Operator MUST maintain a data inventory naming every data category + processor + retention period. Without inventory the operator cannot answer a Subject Access Request in 30 days.

rationale.source: GDPR Art. 30 (light interpretation for solo scale)

rule.rationale: GDPR Art. 30 (light interpretation for solo scale) — anchors Gdpr For Solo Saas rule `data-inventory-required`.

rule.statement: Each data category MUST declare a lawful basis (consent, contract, legitimate-interest, legal-obligation). Bare 'we collect emails' fails the test.
rule.rationale: GDPR Art. 6 — anchors Gdpr For Solo Saas rule `lawful-basis-per-category`.

rule.statement: Every external data processor MUST have a DPA on file (Stripe / Postmark / Plausible publish standard DPAs). Untracked processors = unknowable risk.
rule.rationale: GDPR Art. 28 — anchors Gdpr For Solo Saas rule `processor-dpa-tracked`.

rule.statement: Operator MUST have a documented breach-response plan reaching notification within 72h. The plan can be a one-pager but it must exist.
rule.rationale: GDPR Art. 33 — anchors Gdpr For Solo Saas rule `breach-response-72h`.

rule.statement: Operator MUST name the lawyer-up triggers (EU revenue >€100k, sensitive category, enterprise-DPA-request). When a trigger fires, the methodology stops and counsel takes over.
rule.rationale: Pragmatic compliance practice — anchors Gdpr For Solo Saas rule `lawyer-up-triggers-named`.

rule.statement: If special-category data, regulated B2B vertical, or EU revenue >€100k applies → STOP. Methodology is insufficient; route to enterprise GDPR or specialised methodology.
rule.rationale: Methodology-applicability discipline — anchors Gdpr For Solo Saas rule `skip-this-methodology`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
